title: Chat Control 1.0 sneaks through the EU Parliament, letting companies scan user data without warrants legal tactic used to force a majority-required re-vote on eve of Parliament break
summary: The EU Parliament quietly passed the Chat Control 1.0 bill, allowing companies to scan user data without warrants. This was done through a strategic move that forced a re-vote on the eve of the Parliament break. The bill now requires a re-vote, which may be challenging to achieve.
insight: EU citizens' data privacy is at risk of erosion without adequate safeguards.

title: Micron lifts U.S. spending to $250 billion company takes $500 million position in America's only 300 mm wafer plant
summary: Micron invests $3 billion in US semiconductor supply chain, with $500 million going to GlobalWafers' 300 mm wafer plant.
insight: Boosts US semiconductor self-sufficiency and reduces reliance on foreign suppliers.

title: Samsung readies Gaia AI accelerator for PCs HP and Lenovo are reportedly validating the NPU
summary: Samsung is reportedly preparing its Gaia AI accelerator for PCs, with HP and Lenovo testing it.
insight: Accelerating AI in PCs with improved performance and efficiency

title: Minecraft shown running on Game Boy Color and Game Boy in 3D with textures developer coaxed 3D look out of barely-there hardware
summary: A developer successfully ran Minecraft on the Game Boy Color and Game Boy in 3D with textures, showcasing the system's capabilities despite its limitations.
insight: Optimizing games for underpowered hardware yields creative problem-solving and impressive results.

title: TeamGroup G70 Pro 2TB SSD Review: Low latency meets affordable DRAM
summary: The TeamGroup G70 Pro offers high-end performance at an affordable price, but its poor power efficiency holds it back.
insight: High-end performance at affordable price, but power efficiency is lacking.

title: Redditor buys suspicious drives on eBay just to report the scamming sellers if they get a fake SSD or HDD latest '16TB' find has weights and microSD card hot-glued inside the enclosure to make it feel legit
summary: A Redditor buys suspicious drives on eBay to report scamming sellers, getting them banned and a refund for the purchase.
insight: Reporting fake sellers delays their return to scam unsuspecting victims temporarily.

title: Ingenious father fixes dead RTX 3070 with a jerry-rigged capacitor from an old radio Saves worried son $120 in repair costs, GPU 'works better than before' now
summary: A Russian father fixes his son's dead RTX 3070 with a salvaged capacitor, saving $120 in repair costs. The GPU now works better than before. This resourceful fix showcases DIY ingenuity.
insight: Creative repurposing of parts can lead to affordable, effective solutions.

title: While the U.S. flip-flops on chip sanctions, China is building its own chip supply market export controls are creating conditions for a Sino-Russian chip trade alliance
summary: The US is indecisive on export controls for Chinese chips while China develops its own domestic supply chain, potentially creating an opportunity for a Sino-Russian chip trade alliance.
insight: China's domestic chip supply chain undermines US export control effectiveness.

title: AMD revives aging Zen 2 processor for budget PCs Ryzen 7 4700LE resurfaces in a new $800 RTX 3050 prebuilt
summary: AMD revives Ryzen 7 4700LE in a budget gaming desktop prebuilt with RTX 3050, priced at $799.99. This marks another instance of AMD repurposing older processors. The move targets budget-conscious gamers seeking performance at an affordable price.
insight: Budget gamers get high-end GPU performance paired with older CPU value

title: Intel preps 28-core Nova Lake-S CPUs for Dunlow workstation platform Entry-level Xeon chip features LGA1954 socket
summary: Intel is preparing a 28-core Xeon 'Dunlow' CPU for entry-level servers and workstations. This processor will feature an LGA1954 socket. It's part of Intel's Nova Lake-S CPU lineup.
insight: High core counts for entry-level workstations increase productivity and efficiency.

title: Professor suspected AI-powered cheating on take-home midterms, makes finals in-person only two students scored within 10% of their midterm score
summary: A Brown University professor suspected AI cheating on take-home midterms, with only two students maintaining their high scores in in-person finals.
insight: Academic integrity is compromised when AI-enabled cheating goes undetected for extended periods.

title: Sega s $5M investment saved Nvidia in 1996, now Jensen Huang is heading to Tokyo to mark 30 years of partnership Akihabara event will include a GeForce RTX 5090 FE lottery, an RTX Spark presentation, and more
summary: Nvidia and Sega are celebrating their 30-year partnership with an event in Tokyo. The Akihabara event will feature a lottery for the GeForce RTX 5090 FE, an RTX Spark presentation, and more. This marks a significant milestone in the companies' history.
insight: Nvidia and Sega's partnership has endured for three decades.

title: Alienware AW3426DW gaming monitor review: Premium gaming and OLED goodness in a value-priced package
summary: The Alienware AW3426DW offers a premium gaming experience with its 34-inch OLED display, featuring WQHD resolution, HDR500, and a 280Hz refresh rate.
insight: Value-priced package for high-end gaming and OLED technology

title: AMD's upcoming Zen 6 Medusa Point 10-core APU pops up on Geekbench chip is faster than Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 & even Ryzen AI Max+ 395
summary: A 10-core Zen 6 Medusa Point APU engineering sample outperformed competitors on Geekbench.
insight: AMD's upcoming APU shows significant performance improvements over existing Ryzen models.

title: Elon Musk receives FTC greenlight to buy Mesh Optical as interconnects emerge as AI's tightest bottleneck the move will expand Musk's growing stack of critical AI infrastructure
summary: Elon Musk receives FTC clearance to buy Mesh Optical, completing his AI stack. This acquisition fills the missing layer between Terafab's chips and Gigasat's satellites, addressing the tightening interconnect AI bottleneck.
insight: Closing interconnect bottleneck crucial for AI infrastructure expansion

title: M5Stack Stamp-C5 A Tiny ESP32-C5 USB-C board/module with up to 19 GPIO, 3.7V battery support
summary: The M5Stack Stamp-C5 is a tiny wireless IoT module with ESP32-C5 MCU, offering 19 GPIO pins and 3.7V battery support, suitable for various IoT applications.
insight: Small, versatile module empowers IoT projects with Wi-Fi and Bluetooth connectivity.

title: Open Book Touch A DRM-free, WiFi-connected 4.26-inch open-source hardware e-reader (Crowdfunding)
summary: The Open Book Touch is a DRM-free, open-source e-reader with Wi-Fi connectivity and a 4.26-inch e-paper display.
insight: Accessible, affordable e-reading with no proprietary digital rights management restrictions.

title: Meta tests super sensing AI smart glasses amid privacy concerns
summary: Meta is testing an AI-powered feature for its smart glasses, which could provide a personal assistant always available. The software update aims to enhance user experience but raises concerns about data privacy. Users may have limited control over the flow of personal data.
insight: AI-powered personal assistants raise significant data privacy risks.

title: Best of Sensors: Teradar Summit clinches best of Auto & Mobile
summary: Teradar Summit is the world's first commercial terahertz vision sensor designed to provide unmatched clarity and range under all conditions, winning the Best of Sensors award in the automotive category.
insight: Advanced sensors enhance vehicle safety and performance in all environments.

title: Redditor's latest fake-drive purchase reveals a 60GB microSD card inside a "16TB SSD"
summary: A Redditor buys a '16TB SSD' containing a 60GB microSD card, reports it to eBay, and gets a refund.
insight: Exposing scams helps protect others and prevents further fraudulent activity online.

title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced: 50 GPU Benchmark
summary: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced's GPU demands vary greatly across different settings and hardware configurations. A comprehensive benchmark of 50 graphics cards showcases the game's performance at multiple resolutions, ray tracing modes, and upscaling presets. This detailed analysis helps gamers understand the game's hardware requirements and make informed purchasing decisions.
insight: GPU performance disparity significantly affects Black Flag Resynced's frame rates.

title: Space Marine 2 sold million of copies, and now Saber Interactive has to turn down projects
summary: Saber Interactive has turned down projects due to high demand from Warhammer 40,000: Space Marine 2's success, which has made the studio a leading choice for developing games around established IPs.
insight: Saber Interactive's success creates a high barrier to entry for new collaborations.

title: Cash App reaches $45 million settlement over alleged failure to protect users from fraud
summary: Cash App reaches $45 million settlement after allegations it failed to protect users from fraud, despite promising advanced security measures.
insight: Cash App's failure to protect users from fraud undermines user trust.

title: Nvidia launches free GeForce trading card collection featuring 14 designs from NV1 to RTX 2080 Ti
summary: Nvidia launches free trading card collection to celebrate GeForce's 30-year history.
insight: Nvidia honors legacy as AI future shifts GPU landscape focus.

title: Minecraft joins Doom in the "can it run?" hall of fame with Game Boy Color port
summary: Minecraft has been successfully ported to the Game Boy Color by Game of Tobi. This is an impressive feat, as Minecraft was not originally designed for the device. The port showcases Tobi's expertise in getting games running on unexpected hardware.
insight: Minecraft's Game Boy Color port pushes handheld console capabilities

title: DLSS Swapper creator issues malware warning over user-submitted DLLs
summary: The DLSS Swapper creator warns of malware risks from user-submitted DLLs. These malicious files are being uploaded through the program's design. Users are advised to exercise caution.
insight: User-submitted DLLs pose a significant malware risk to DLSS Swapper users.

title: New York becomes the first state to ban smart glasses in courtrooms
summary: New York becomes the first state to ban smart glasses in courtrooms, applying to all types, including those with prescription lenses, for everyone entering the building.
insight: Courtroom integrity preserved with ban on recording technology

title: Interpol arrests 5,811 people, seizes $293 million in global scammer crackdown
summary: Interpol arrested 5,811 people and seized $293 million in a global scammer crackdown, highlighting the scale of the problem.
insight: The scale of international scams is staggering and requires coordinated global action.

title: After losing about $1 billion in Reaper drones over Iran, the US wants a disposable alternative
summary: The US Defense Innovation Unit seeks a disposable aircraft alternative to Reaper drones, which cost $1 billion lost over Iran. The Massed Modular Aircraft (MMA) project invites commercial tech submissions for the solution. This innovation aims to reduce military drone expenses.
insight: Pentagon seeks cost-effective drone alternatives after significant financial losses.

title: Professor's chart exposes the scale of AI cheating in college exams
summary: A Brown University professor discovered widespread AI cheating in his class, revealing the scale of the issue in academia.
insight: Generative AI is compromising the validity of academic assessments and knowledge.

title: Budget smartphones are the next victim of the memory crisis
summary: Mid-to-low-end smartphones are struggling with rising memory costs, leading to a 22% YoY decline. The thin profit margins make it difficult for manufacturers to absorb these costs. This trend is expected to continue due to the memory crisis.
insight: Thinner profit margins leave budget phones vulnerable to memory price hikes

title: Microsoft tells Obsidian to pause or cancel Avowed 2 and make a new Fallout game
summary: Microsoft directs Obsidian to cancel or pause Avowed 2, focus on new Fallout game. The project would be led by Josh Sawyer, Fallout: New Vegas director. Sawyer's expertise brings a familiar hand to the Fallout franchise.
insight: Microsoft prioritizes Fallout over Obsidian's internal Avowed series ambitions
